assignment_description:
Stage 13: IN BRITANNIA (Infinitives and Conjugations)
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook, sticky notes

PREP: Preview Stage 13 in Student Text and Teacher's Manual. Read "Opening Page" and "Model Sentences" Teacher's Manual p.4-6.

➜ INTRO
View the picture on p.1. How do the house and the people's clothes look different from those in Pompeii? Why do you think the houses were built differently?

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.22 with sticky notes. Copy the Latin words in "Vocabulary Checklist 13." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 13.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences p.2-4
Narrate what is understood in English. Translate each sentence orally.

• Why do you think the slaves do not want to work?
• Discuss the characters and what you learned about them in the model sentences.

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
• Map p.16-17: Look at the place names. What is the same now? What is different? What kinds of structures would have been needed to keep control of such a large area? What percentage of Roman citizens would you guess actually lived in Rome?
• Read "Life in the empire" p.16.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"About the Language 1: infinitives" paragraph 1 p.10

➜ PRACTICE
Look back at the Model Sentences (p.2-4) and find 6 infinitives. Record them and their meanings in Latin notebook. (See Answer Key Supplement p.1 to check.)

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 13 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

➜ DISCUSS
• How is this household different from that of Caecilius?
• Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

There is also an “Answer Key Supplement" that can be downloaded from the Latin 2 (Independent Study) Quick Links. Some assignments will direct you to check translations or answers with this resource.
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER TIP
The translations for all the model sentences and stories are in the digital Cambridge Unit 2 Teacher's Resource document, "Student's Book Translations and Answers." The answers to "About the language" and "Practicing the language" exercises are also located here. The document can be downloaded as a single file containing all the Unit 2 translations and answers or as individual files for each stage. Use this to check your translations and assignments unless otherwise specified.

assignment_description:
Stage 13: Trēs Servī
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

PREP: Read "trēs servī" Teacher's Manual p.7 and work through the lesson before teaching.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
View the picture on p.5. What is the mood of the slaves? Who is in this skit? What does each person do? Where is each person from?

➜ PREVIEW
Vocabulary words p.5

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"trēs servī" p.5
Narrate what is understood in English.

• Now read a dramatized reading of "trēs servī" and translate each part orally. Use the glossary as needed.

➜ DISCUSS
How did the mood of the slaves change in this story? What might these slaves experience as a result of Salvius' being attacked?

➜ PRACTICE
• Find at least four infinitives in the story. List them and their meanings in Latin notebook. (See Answer Key Supplement p.1 to check.)
• Complete paragraph 2 of "About the Language 1: infinitives" p.10.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 13 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

Stage 13: Coniūrātiō
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

PREP: Read "coniūrātiō" Teacher's Manual p.7-9. Work through the lesson before teaching.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
What metal tools do you remember from Unit 1? Where does metal come from? This story takes place in a mine.

➜ PREVIEW
Vocabulary words p.6

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"coniūrātiō" p.6
Narrate what is understood in English.

• Reread lines 1-10. Translate each sentence orally using the glossary when needed.

➜ PRACTICE
Can you find any infinitives in lines 1-21? (See Answer Key Supplement p.2 to check.)

➜ DISCUSS
Share some information from "Further information" Teacher's Manual p.8-9, and discuss.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 13 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

Stage 13: Coniūrātiō, Mining and Farming, and Bregāns
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

PREP: Read "Mining and farming" and "Bregāns" Teacher's Manual p.9-11.

➜ RECAP
• Review infinitive endings from the last few lessons. We will see one more today.
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook and vocabulary words on p.6.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"coniūrātiō" p.6

• Reread lines 1-21 orally. Narrate.
• Read lines 22-end. Translate each sentence orally using the glossary when needed.

➜ PRACTICE
What infinitive is used in lines 22-the end? What is the ending? (See Answer Key Supplement p.2 to check.)

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"About the Language 1: infinitives" paragraph 3 p.10

➜ PRACTICE
Complete the exercise at the bottom of p.10 (a-h).

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Mining and farming" p.7
Examine the pictures.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 13 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

Stage 13: Bregāns and Verb Tenses
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ PREVIEW
Vocabulary words p.8

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Bregāns" p.8
Read orally. Translate each sentence orally using the glossary when needed.

• Answer the questions on p.9 orally.

➜ READ, DISCUSS, & PRACTICE
• Study Present, Imperfect, Perfect, and Pluperfect tenses on p.168. Discuss observations about word endings and their translations.
• Read "Persons and endings" on p.169.
• Complete Exercises 1-3 at the top of p.169, using p.168-169 to help. (See Answer Key Supplement p.2 to check.)

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 13 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

➜ DISCUSS
Which words from the last few lessons look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 13: Salvius, Rufilla, Infinitives, and Salvius Fundum Inspicit
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

PREP: Read "Salvius fundum īnspicit" Teacher's Manual p.12-13.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
View the picture on p.13.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Salvius" and "Rufilla" p.18-19

• What kind of relationship do you think Salvius and Rufilla might have had? How might she have felt about moving to Britannia?
• Why would the estate owner want to inspect the fields? How do you think the slaves might feel as their master approaches to inspect their work?
• What do you think about the skill of Salvius as an estate owner and Vārica as an overseer?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"About the language 1: infinitives" paragraphs 4-5 p.11

➜ PRACTICE
Translate the examples in paragraph 6 on p.11 using the glossary as needed. (See Answer Key Supplement p.3 to check.)

➜ PREVIEW
Vocabulary words p.12

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Salvius fundum īnspicit" p.12
Narrate what is understood in English.

• Reread lines 1-13 slowly, translating orally. Use the glossary as needed.
• Answer the first eight questions on p.12 of Teacher's Manual orally. (See Answer Key Supplement p.3 to check.)

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 13 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

Stage 13: Salvius Fundum Inspicit, Britannia, and Practicing the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

PREP: Read "Cultural context material" Teacher's Manual p.14-17.

➜ RECAP
• Review what happened in lines 1-13 of "Salvius fundum īnspicit" on p.12.
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
View the map and pictures on p.21.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Salvius fundum īnspicit" lines 14-end p.12
Reread slowly. Translate orally using the glossary as needed.

• Answer the last four questions on p.12 of Teacher's Manual. (See Answer Key Supplement p.3 to check.)

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Britannia" p.20

• How were the lives of house slaves and field slaves different? Why do you think they were so different?
• Share some information from "Cultural context material" on p.14-16 of Teacher's Manual.
• How do you think Celtic civilization changed after the Roman invasion?

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercises 1 and 2 of "Practicing the Language" on p.14. Use p.170 and glossary as needed.

Stage 13: Grammar Practice
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"About the language 2: -que" paragraphs 1-2 p.13

• Complete the exercise in paragraph 1 orally.

➜ PRACTICE
• Translate the examples in paragraph 3 on p.13.
• Complete Exercise 3 of "Practicing the language" on p.15. Use the glossary and p.168 as needed.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Part Two: Vocabulary" paragraphs 6-7 p.179

➜ PRACTICE
• Create a table with four columns in the Latin notebook. Label the columns: First Person Singular, Infinitive, First Person Singular Perfect, and Conjugation #.
• Place the words and their meanings from paragraphs 7-9 (p.179) on the table in the correct columns. Complete the table using the glossary as needed. Use p.168 as a reference. (See Answer Key Supplement p.4 to check.)

Stage 14 - APUD SALVIUM (Adjectives, Prepositional Phrases, Ablative Case)

PREP: Read "Opening page," "Model Sentences," and "Rūfilla" Teacher's Manual p.19-21.

➜ NOTE
Students may enjoy revisiting Our Island Story Ch.2-6 during Stage 14.

➜ INTRO

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.42 with sticky notes. Copy the Latin words in "Vocabulary Checklist 14." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 14.
.
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences p.24
Narrate what is understood in English. Reread dramatically.

➜ PREVIEW
Vocabulary words p.25

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Rūfilla" p.25
Narrate what is understood in English. Use the glossary as needed to understand the meaning.

• Assign parts and practice reading aloud. Perform the skit once in English and once in Latin. Expression should demonstrate comprehension.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 14 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

➜ DISCUSS

• Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 14: Romanization of a Province and Domitilla Cubiculum Parat 1
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook, Cambridge Unit 1

PREP: Read "Domitilla cubiculum parat I" Teacher's Manual p.21-22. Review dative case in Unit 1, Stage 9, p.120-21.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Romanization of a province" p.37

➜ PRACTICE
• Copy lines 4-5 of "Rufilla" on p.25. Translate them into English and then back into Latin. Use the glossary as needed and pay attention to the verb tenses. Check Latin translations against the originals; correct any mistakes. (See Answer Key Supplement p.5 to check.)
• Optional: Review dative case (Cambridge Latin Unit 1, Stage 9, p.120-121).

➜ PREVIEW
Vocabulary words p.26 - top of p.27

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Domitilla cubiculum parat I" p.26-27
Narrate what is understood in English.

• Reread slowly. Translate orally using the glossary as needed.

➜ DISCUSS
What do you think about Domitilla?

➜ WRITE
Narrate definitions of nominative, accusative, and dative cases in Latin notebook. Give examples from today's reading. (See Answer Key Supplement p.5 to check.)

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 14 or the glossary beginning on p.180.

Stage 14: Domitilla Cubiculum Parat II and Adjectives
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

PREP: Read "Domitilla cubiculum parat II" and "About the language 1: adjectives" Teacher's Manual p.22-23.

Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

Vocabulary words p.27

➜ DISCUSS

➜ PRACTICE
In "Domitilla cubiculum parat II" on p.27, find at least one example of nominative and accusative cases of nouns and at least two examples of infinitives. Copy and translate the sentences. Underline the nominative, accusative, and infinitive words and their translations. (See Answer Key Supplement p.5 to check.)

There is also an “Answer Key Supplement" that can be downloaded from the Latin 2 (Independent Study) Quick Links. Some assignments will direct you to check translations or answers with this resource.

assignment_teachersNote:
★ Latin 2 (Independent Study) — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 2: Translate the following into English. Identify the parts of speech for the words in brackets. Then, without looking at these Latin sentences, translate your English sentences back into Latin.
1. servī epistulās [longās] scribēbant.
2. Philus pecūniam [numerāre] potest.
3. Vārica [servōs] ancillāsque īnspexit.
4. dominus servō fessō praemium [dedit].

Answers:
1. servī epistulās longās scribēbant. The slaves were writing long letters. (adjective)
2. Philus pecūniam numerāre potest. Philus is able to count money. (infinitive verb)
3. Vārica servōs ancillāsque īnspexit. Varica inspects the slaves and the girl slaves. (plural noun)
4. dominus servō fessō praemium dedit. The master gave the tired slave a reward. (verb) OR The master gave a reward to the tired slave.

· Cambridge Unit 2: Translate the following sentences into English. Then translate them back into Latin. 
1. rēx, quī scēptrum tenēbat, in ātriō sedēbat.
2. cēna, quam Volūbilis parābat, erat splendida.
3. servī sōlem vidēre nōn poterant.
4. nōs redīre nōlēbāmus.
5. Dumnorix nāvem amīcis ostendit.

Answers:
1. Rēx, quī scēptrum tenēbat, in ātriō sedēbat. The king, who was holding a scepter, was sitting in the hall.
2. cēna, quam Volūbilis parābat, erat splendida. The dinner, which Volubilis was preparing, was splendid.
3. servī sōlem vidēre nōn poterant. The slaves were not able to see the sun.
4. nōs redīre nōlēbāmus. We were not wanting to return.
5. Dumnorix nāvem amīcis ostendit. Dumnorix showed the ship to the friends.

assignment_teachersNote:
★ Latin 2 (Independent Study) — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 2: Translate the following into English. Identify the parts of speech for the words in brackets. Then, without looking at these Latin sentences, translate your English sentences back into Latin.
1. servī epistulās [longās] scribēbant.
2. Philus pecūniam [numerāre] potest.
3. Vārica [servōs] ancillāsque īnspexit.
4. dominus servō fessō praemium [dedit].

Answers:
1. servī epistulās longās scribēbant. The slaves were writing long letters. (adjective)
2. Philus pecūniam numerāre potest. Philus is able to count money. (infinitive verb)
3. Vārica servōs ancillāsque īnspexit. Varica inspects the slaves and the girl slaves. (plural noun)
4. dominus servō fessō praemium dedit. The master gave the tired slave a reward. (verb) OR The master gave a reward to the tired slave.

· Cambridge Unit 2: Translate the following sentences into English. Then translate them back into Latin. 
1. rēx, quī scēptrum tenēbat, in ātriō sedēbat.
2. cēna, quam Volūbilis parābat, erat splendida.
3. servī sōlem vidēre nōn poterant.
4. nōs redīre nōlēbāmus.
5. Dumnorix nāvem amīcis ostendit.

Answers:
1. Rēx, quī scēptrum tenēbat, in ātriō sedēbat. The king, who was holding a scepter, was sitting in the hall.
2. cēna, quam Volūbilis parābat, erat splendida. The dinner, which Volubilis was preparing, was splendid.
3. servī sōlem vidēre nōn poterant. The slaves were not able to see the sun.
4. nōs redīre nōlēbāmus. We were not wanting to return.
5. Dumnorix nāvem amīcis ostendit. Dumnorix showed the ship to the friends.

Stage 17: Review and Catch Up
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 2, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
• Review Quīntus’ journey thus far in Stage 17.
• Review Vocabulary Checklist 17 words in Latin notebook.

➜ WRITE
Compose a written Latin narration about what Quīntus has done in Stage 17. Keep the sentences simple to avoid using complex grammar. Try your best to think in Latin. A good starting place might be “Quīntus ad Alexandriam pervenit.” (See Answer Key Supplement p.18-19 for a sample narration.)

➜ EVALUATE
Perform the following grammatical checks on your written Latin narration:
\- Does the subject of the sentence match the verb? For example, if the subject is singular, then the verb needs to be singular.
\- Do the verb tenses make sense? Are they consistent? Is everything happening in the past (perfect or imperfect tense) or the present?
\- Case check: Do the subjects have nominative endings? Do the direct objects have accusative endings? Do the nouns that show possession have genitive endings? Do the indirect objects have dative endings?

➜ CATCH UP
Use any additional time to catch up on unfinished work from Stage 17.

assignment_teachersNote:
★ Latin 2 (Independent Study) — TERM 2 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 2: Complete each sentence with the correct form in the parentheses.
 A. In viīs......erant multī mercātōres. (urbis, urbium)
B. Faber per fenestram......spectābat. (casārum, casae)
C. Barbille! Nōnne dē monumentīs audīre......? (vīs, vult)
D. Multī virī fēminaeque ad templum contendunt, quod sacrificium vidēre......(vult, volunt)
E. Paucī sumus. Aegyptiōs superāre nōn ........ (possum, possumus)
F. ........tabernāriī Eutychō pecūniam dedērunt. (multī, multae)
G. Quīntus templum ......... vīsitāvit. (magnificam, magnificum)
H. In templō, ....... prope tabernam Clēmentis erat, habitābat fēlēs sacra. (qui, quae, quod)

Answers:
A. urbis B. casae C. vīs D. volunt E. possumus F. multī G. magnificum H. quod

· Cambridge Unit 2: Translate lines 1-6 of “taberna” on p.95 into English.

Translation:
After we arrived at the city, I searched a long time for a store for Clemens. At last Barbillus, who owned many buildings, offered me a very good store. This store was near the Temple of the goddess Isis. In this part of the city is a street, in which all the storekeepers sell glass. The store, which Barbillus was offering me, had an excellent location. Barbillus, however, was doubtful.

· Cambridge Unit 2: Identify the case (NOM/GEN/DAT/ACC/ABL) of the following words in "taberna" on p.95:
a. Barbillus (line 2)
b. multa aedificia (line 2) 
c. deae (line 4)
d. urbis (line 4)
e. vitrum (line 5)

Answers:
a. NOM b. ACC c. GEN d. GEN e. ACC

Compose a written Latin narration about Aristō in Stage 19. Keep the sentences simple to avoid using complex grammar. Try your best to think in Latin. A good starting place might be “Aristō est miser vir.” (See Answer Key Supplement p.28 for an example.)

➜ EVALUATE
Perform the following grammatical checks on your written Latin narration:
\- Does the subject of the sentence match the verb? For example, if the subject is singular, the verb must be singular.
\- Do the verb tenses make sense? Are they consistent? Is everything happening in the past (perfect or imperfect tense) or the present?
\- Case check: Do the subjects have nominative endings? Do the direct objects have accusative endings? Do the nouns that show possession have genitive endings? Do the indirect objects have dative endings?

There is also an “Answer Key Supplement" that can be downloaded from the Latin 2 (Independent Study) Quick Links. Some assignments will direct you to check translations or answers with this resource.

assignment_teachersNote:
★ Latin 2 (Independent Study) — TERM 3 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 2: Complete each sentence with the correct form in the parentheses.
a. Mercātor fīliam ....... laudāvit. (bonus, bona, bonum, bonam)
b. Pater uxōrī ........ pecūniam dedit. (bonus, bona, bonō, bonae)
c. Rēx mīlitēs ........ salūtāvit. (fortis, fortēs, fortibus)
d. Aristō iuvenēs versum ...... audīvit. (recitāns, recitantem, recitantēs)
e. Barbillus, dē vītā ........ , Quīntum arcessīvit. (dēspērāns, dēspērantēs)
f. Hippopotamum ....... nōn cōnspexī. (adveniēns, advenientem)
g. Barbillus: “Quīnte! mēcum ad vēnātiōnem ..........!” (venī, venīte)
h. Quīntus: “Servī ......... hippopotamum vexāre!” (nōlī, nōlīte)

Answers:
a. bonam b. bonae c. fortēs d. recitantēs e. dēspērāns f. advenientem g. venī h. nōlīte

· Cambridge Unit 2: Translate “astrologus victor II” lines 4-7 (“in somniō”....”nārrāvit”) on p.141.

Translation:
In the dream, I was walking through the streets of the city of Alexandria. Suddenly I saw a boy standing in the road. The boy was your slave, whom the Egyptians killed in a riot. He told me about some very special ointment.

· Cambridge Unit 2: Identify the case (NOM/GEN/DAT/ACC/ABL) of the following nouns or adjectives in "astrologus victor II" on p.141: 
a. urbis (line 4)
b. puerum (line 5) 
c. tuus (line 5)
d. Aegyptiī (line 6)
e. mihi (line 6)

Answers:
a. GEN b. ACC c. NOM d. NOM e. DAT
